title: psyllium phaeostomum
intro: Psyllium phaeostomum is a plant-based species of the genus Plantago, commonly known as psyllium, which is a type of herb with medicinal properties. The plant is known for its high fiber content, which is often used to treat digestive issues such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Medicinally, psyllium phaeostomum has been used to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar levels, and support healthy gut bacteria. Herbal preparations of psyllium phaeostomum can be found in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and powdered supplements, and can also be consumed as a dietary fiber supplement in the form of psyllium husk.
